# Module 0: Getting Started and Data Management {.unnumbered}

## Module 0.1 – Lab Materials and Equipment Overview

This table lists all materials and equipment required across Myco-Ed modules. Use the search box to filter by module, item, company, or catalog number (web view), or scroll through the complete table below (in PDF and downloads).

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
materials = [
  // Module 1
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "100×15 mm Petri dishes", description: "Petri dishes for fungal culturing", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "Bacteriological agar (e.g., Difco Bacto Agar)", description: "Agar base for culture media", company: "Difco/BD", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "Autoclave", description: "Sterilization of media and equipment", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "Laminar flow hood", description: "Sterile work environment (optional if unavailable)", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "Forceps", description: "Sample manipulation", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "Scalpels", description: "Cutting leaf tissue", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "150 mL beaker", description: "Container for ethanol", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "Parafilm", description: "Sealing Petri plates", company: "Bemis", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "Gloves", description: "Personal protection", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "Hole punch (all metal)", description: "Cutting leaf discs; scalpel is an acceptable substitute", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "Kimwipes", description: "Lab wipes", company: "Kimtech", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "Paper bags", description: "Field sample storage", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "10% bleach (in autoclaved water)", description: "Surface sterilization solution", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "70% ethanol", description: "Surface sterilization", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 1 – Fungal Sampling & Culturing", item: "Paper towels", description: "General lab use", company: "", catalog: "" },
  // Module 2
  { module: "Module 2 – Fungal Isolation", item: "60×15 mm Petri dishes", description: "Smaller dishes for fungal isolation transfers", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 2 – Fungal Isolation", item: "Potato Dextrose Agar (PDA)", description: "Selective culture media for fungi", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 2 – Fungal Isolation", item: "Autoclave", description: "Sterilization of media and equipment", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 2 – Fungal Isolation", item: "Laminar flow hood", description: "Sterile work environment (optional if unavailable)", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 2 – Fungal Isolation", item: "Gloves", description: "Personal protection", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 2 – Fungal Isolation", item: "Sharpie or lab marker (fine tip)", description: "Plate labeling", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 2 – Fungal Isolation", item: "70% ethanol", description: "Surface sterilization", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 2 – Fungal Isolation", item: "Kimwipes", description: "Lab wipes", company: "Kimtech", catalog: "" },
  // Module 3
  { module: "Module 3 – Phenotypic Assays", item: "Varies by assay", description: "Materials depend on the chosen phenotypic assay", company: "", catalog: "" },
  // Module 4
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Gloves", description: "Personal protection", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Sharpie", description: "Tube and plate labeling", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Thermal cycler", description: "PCR amplification", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Vortex", description: "Sample mixing", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Centrifuge", description: "Centrifugation of PCR and microfuge tubes", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Heat block / thermomixer", description: "For 1.5–2.0 mL microfuge tubes", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes (DNA LoBind)", description: "Low-bind tubes to minimize DNA adsorption", company: "Eppendorf", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "0.2 mL PCR tubes", description: "PCR reactions", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Microfuge and PCR tube racks", description: "Tube organization", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Labeling tape", description: "Sample labeling", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Monarch Spin gDNA Extraction Kit (100 reactions)", description: "Spin-column genomic DNA extraction kit", company: "New England BioLabs", catalog: "T3010S" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "MetaPolyzyme, DNA free", description: "Enzyme blend for fungal cell wall digestion", company: "Millipore Sigma", catalog: "MAC4LDF-5X1VL" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "TE Buffer", description: "DNA storage and dilution buffer", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "CTAB solution (100 mL)", description: "Alternative DNA extraction reagent", company: "Promega", catalog: "MC1411" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Parafilm", description: "Tube sealing", company: "Bemis", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "70% ethanol", description: "Surface sterilization", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Micropipettors (1–10, 20–200, 100–1000 µL)", description: "Liquid handling", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Micropipette tips (1–10, 20–200, 100–1000 µL, filtered preferred)", description: "Filtered tips reduce contamination risk", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Kimwipes", description: "Lab wipes", company: "Kimtech", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "PCR primers: ITS1f, ITS4, 5.8SR, fITS7", description: "Fungal ITS barcoding primers; normalize to 100 µM in TE buffer. ITS1f: CTTGGTCATTTAGAGGAAGTAA; ITS4: TCCTCCGCTTATTGATATGC; 5.8SR: CGCTGCGTTCTTCATCG; fITS7: GTGARTCATCGAATCTTTG", company: "IDT or similar", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix (100 reactions)", description: "High-fidelity PCR amplification master mix", company: "New England BioLabs", catalog: "M0494S" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "PCR water", description: "Nuclease-free water for PCR setup", company: "New England BioLabs", catalog: "B1500S" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Exo-CIP Rapid PCR Cleanup Kit (100 reactions)", description: "Enzymatic cleanup of PCR products prior to sequencing", company: "New England BioLabs", catalog: "E1050S" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "E-Gel system with 1.2% agarose gels", description: "Pre-cast gel electrophoresis system", company: "ThermoFisher", catalog: "G4010010" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "E-Gel Sizing DNA Ladder", description: "DNA size standard for gel electrophoresis", company: "ThermoFisher", catalog: "10488100" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Qubit Fluorometer with dsDNA HS Assay Kit", description: "Fluorometric DNA quantification", company: "ThermoFisher", catalog: "Q32851" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing", item: "Sanger sequencing service access", description: "Capillary sequencing of ITS amplicons (e.g., Genewiz, Eurofins, Eton)", company: "Various", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing (long-read, optional)", item: "ONT Rapid Barcoding Kit v14", description: "Long-read nanopore sequencing library preparation", company: "Oxford Nanopore Technologies", catalog: "SQK-RBK114.24" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing (long-read, optional)", item: "MinION sequencing device", description: "Portable nanopore sequencer", company: "Oxford Nanopore Technologies", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing (long-read, optional)", item: "MinION flow cells (R10.4.1)", description: "Nanopore sequencing flow cells", company: "Oxford Nanopore Technologies", catalog: "FLO-MIN114" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing (long-read, optional)", item: "Ultrapure BSA 50 mg/mL", description: "Blocking agent for long-read library prep", company: "ThermoFisher", catalog: "AM2616" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing (long-read, optional)", item: "Magnetic rack for 1.5 mL tubes (DynaMag-2)", description: "Bead-based cleanup during long-read library preparation", company: "ThermoFisher", catalog: "12321D" },
  { module: "Module 4 – DNA Sequencing (long-read, optional)", item: "Nuclease-free water and 100% ethanol", description: "For preparing 80% ethanol wash during bead cleanup", company: "", catalog: "" },
  // Module 5
  { module: "Module 5 – Tissue Preparation", item: "Cellophane discs (autoclaved in gauze)", description: "Individually rinsed and autoclaved before use", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 5 – Tissue Preparation", item: "60×15 mm Petri dishes", description: "Culture dishes for tissue preparation", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 5 – Tissue Preparation", item: "Potato Dextrose Agar (PDA)", description: "Fungal culture media", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 5 – Tissue Preparation", item: "Silica beads", description: "For sample desiccation or homogenization", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 5 – Tissue Preparation", item: "Gloves", description: "Personal protection", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 5 – Tissue Preparation", item: "Incubator", description: "Controlled temperature environment for fungal growth", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 5 – Tissue Preparation", item: "Balance", description: "Weighing biomass samples", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 5 – Tissue Preparation", item: "Forceps", description: "Sample manipulation", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 5 – Tissue Preparation", item: "Scalpel", description: "Cutting tissue", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 5 – Tissue Preparation", item: "Small jar with lid or covered plates", description: "Sample containment and storage", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 5 – Tissue Preparation", item: "Pure fungal culture", description: "Starting biological material", company: "", catalog: "" },
  // Module 6
  { module: "Module 6 – Sample Submission", item: "Sample shipment supplies", description: "For shipping samples to AGI (see Module 6 SOP for details)", company: "", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 6 – Sample Submission", item: "MycoEd Metadata Form printout", description: "Paper copy of completed metadata form included with shipment (landscape orientation)", company: "", catalog: "" },
  // Module 7
  { module: "Module 7 – Genomic Analyses", item: "MycoCosm (JGI portal)", description: "Web-based genomic analysis portal", company: "DOE Joint Genome Institute", catalog: "" },
  { module: "Module 7 – Genomic Analyses", item: "Computer with internet access", description: "Required for all web-based genomic analyses", company: "", catalog: "" }
]

viewof search = Inputs.search(materials, {placeholder: "Search items, companies, catalog numbers…"})

Inputs.table(search, {
  columns: ["module", "item", "description", "company", "catalog"],
  header: {
    module: "Module",
    item: "Item",
    description: "Description",
    company: "Company",
    catalog: "Catalog #"
  }
})
```

:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf,docx"}

```{r}
#| echo: false
materials_data <- read.csv("_materials.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
knitr::kable(materials_data)
```

:::


## Module 0.2 – Create iNaturalist Traditional Project Instructions

### iNaturalist Introduction
iNaturalist is a tool that we can use to document the fungal isolates in Myco-Ed courses. This tool allows instructors and students to record the metadata associated with the fungal isolates. Metadata will include plant species, geographic location, elevation, fungal DNA sequences, growth assays, and any other data relevant for this project. This platform will also enable us to track growth of the MycoEd project. The goal of this long-term research project is to develop annotated fungal genomes to better understand fungal biology.

To begin, make sure that you have an iNaturalist account.

### Project Overview
There are three kinds of projects:
1. Umbrella Projects
2. Collection Projects
3. Traditional Projects

The umbrella project for MycoEd is the MycoEd Fungal Genomics Education Project. Each course will establish a Traditional Project in iNaturalist. A traditional project allows us to control whose observations are included in the project, which enables better downstream data management. We ask that your class use the Traditional Project for MycoEd for only your MycoEd fungi. If your course includes a fungal collection using iNaturalist, please have a separate iNaturalist project for these fungal observations.

### Make a MycoEd Traditional Project [Instructor Instructions]
1. Log into iNaturalist.
2. Go to Projects.
3. This will take you to Your Projects.
4. On the bottom right of the page, select Start a Project.
5. Start a Traditional Project. Scroll to the bottom of the page to find the Traditional Project link for this option.

**Note: If you do not have this option, please contact Amy Honan (amy.honan@oregonstate.edu) or Andrew Wilson (andrew.wilson@BotanicGardens.org) for assistance.**

6. Complete the following criteria indicated in the lettered parts of the figure below:
   a. Add Title. Include Institution, term or semester, and year.
   b. Make this Invite Only. You will add your students to this project.
   c. Add images or logos as you see fit. This step is useful if you manage multiple projects.

7. Other fields to consider:
   a. Location: You may choose to limit the locations of your observations. If your project is set to invite only, this is likely unnecessary.
   b. Observation Fields: Here you can link the fungal endophytes back to the host plants. In the future, we may have further instructions for data to add here.

8. Complete the project by selecting the Create button.
9. You may add your MycoEd concierges to your project if you wish (Andrew_wilson, ahonan).
10. Email Andrew Wilson (andrew.wilson@BotanicGardens.org) to be added to the MycoEd Fungal Genomics Education Project.
11. Add your project name to the text highlighted in yellow in copies of the following documents:
    a. Module 1 – Using iNaturalist
    b. Module 1 – Sampling Fungal Endophytes and Culturing Student Instructions

Adding your name to the copies of these documents will enable you to use them directly in your courses.

### Add Participants (=Students) To The Project
Because we've made the Project "Invite Only" you will need to manually invite each of the students to join the course. They are instructed to join the course in Module 1.2.

1. Open your Myco-Ed project.
2. Select the "Members" section on the lower right. In this section you can manage membership and participation for the project.
3. Click on "invite new members here" in the yellow box on the right.
4. Once you've received the iNaturalist User IDs, start adding your students to the blank space under "Invite people…". Their names/user ID should appear in the drop down menu.

If their names do not show up, it is because they have not completed their iNaturalist registration, or the User ID is different from what they've supplied.

5. Instruct the students to look for their invitation and join the project. They must now add their course's iNaturalist observations to the project for them to show up in the project.

Use this last step as a requirement for course credit to ensure the process is completed by students.

## Module 0.3 – iNaturalist Checklist

### iNaturalist Checklist
This checklist will help you track your progress in managing Myco-Ed project data using iNaturalist.

#### For Instructors

- [ ] Create an iNaturalist Account. (Module 0.2)
- [ ] Create an iNaturalist Traditional Project for your course. (Module 0.2) You may need to request the assistance of the Myco-Ed team to create this if you do not have the pre-requisite number of observations.
- [ ] Tell your students the name of the iNaturalist Project, and request their iNaturalist user IDs.
- [ ] Add the students to the course project once they've completed Module 1.2. (Module 0.2)
- [ ] Instruct students on iNaturalist data management obligations for course credit (at the instructor's discretion).

#### For Students/Participants
**Homework Assignment 1 (Module 1.2)**

- [ ] Create an iNaturalist Account.
- [ ] Get the iNaturalist App for your phone
- [ ] Give your instructor your iNaturalist User Name.
- [ ] Update your real name under your account settings.
- [ ] Watch some videos and tutorials.
- [ ] Join the course project once you receive an invitation from the instructor/TA.

**Make an Observation (Module 1.2)**

- [ ] Take multiple images of the plant host (or habitat photos of the environment) and include at least one image with a unique field number. (e.g., MycoEd_004)
- [ ] Add the observation to your course project.
- [ ] Save and upload the observation to the cloud once you're connected to wifi.
- [ ] Add Observation Field for MycoEd ID.
- [ ] Add Observation Field for DNA Barcode ITS once you have DNA sequence data.
- [ ] Update the name of fungal isolates using DNA sequence BLAST results.
- [ ] Add Observation Field for Host Observation for fungal isolates linking to the original host on iNaturalist.
